turns.user_message: I’ve come to the conclusion that I’m just naturally bracing for disappointment in every interaction lately. It’s like a sudden switch flipped a few days ago, and now I’m reading into every little pause or short reply. I catch myself overthinking messages that probably mean absolutely nothing at all. It’s exhausting trying to keep my emotional walls up all the time when I really just want to relax.

This defensive habit has completely taken over my daily routine now. I find myself drafting and deleting replies for hours because I’m terrified of saying the wrong thing. Every interaction feels like walking on thin ice, and I’m constantly bracing for some kind of quiet rejection. It’s frustrating because I know I’m pushing people away before they even get the chance to step closer.

I honestly don’t recognize the person I’ve become lately, and it’s starting to weigh heavily on my chest. I’m so convinced that I’m going to mess everything up that I’ve stopped reaching out entirely. My mind spins in endless loops, replaying every awkward moment and convincing myself I’m fundamentally unapproachable. I just feel completely trapped inside my own head, watching connections slip through my fingers because I’m too scared to just be myself.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This just started recently.
